title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Miss Marple presents a letter written by Letitia to her sister, Charlotte, which details a severe family quarrel involving Sonya, the sister of Randell. According to the letter, Sonya intends to marry a man named Dmitri Stanforth, a move Randell opposes by labeling Stanforth a scoundrel and a fraud. Letitia notes her attempts to mediate the conflict, while another family member, Bella, suggests Sonya is motivated by greed. The letter also mentions that Letitia works in Randell's office and that Charlotte is undergoing iodine therapy.

Following the reading, Miss Marple suggests that Sonya's motives are to secure her interests in "both worlds." Colonel Korduk notes that Letitia's description of Sonya's hand gestures reminds him of someone. The discussion then turns to the revolver used in the crime; Mrs. Round informs the Inspector that, according to her maid Mrs. Bartlett, Captain Eastbrook keeps a revolver in a drawer with his collars.

Miss Marple also presents a note from Miss Blacklock regarding inquiries to be made after three o'clock on a Thursday. Mrs. Round explains this refers to a local system of secret bartering where goods are exchanged on Thursday butter-making days. Miss Blacklock typically used a box under a sideboard for these exchanges, but the box was found empty. The Inspector expresses frustration with the complexity of these local customs and the difficulty of identifying Sonya, noting that the deaths of a man and a woman remain unsolved.
